nodes.name: Time Complexity
nodes.definition:
It is a type of analysis to determine the approximate time required by an
algorithm to complete a given task when the number of inputs increases.
nodes.last_question_asked: If you were to implement a search algorithm that performs a linear scan through a dataset, how would the time complexity change if you instead utilized a hash table, and what does this shift imply about the relationship between data organization and computational efficiency?

nodes.name: Big O notation
nodes.definition:
It describes worst case scenario of an algorithm to complete a task when
input size increases.
nodes.last_question_asked: If you were designing an algorithm to process a massive dataset, why might choosing an implementation with a "better" Big O complexity sometimes lead to slower actual performance than a "worse" one, and what specific factors about the hardware or the nature of your input might cause this discrepancy?
